chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ جَاوَزَ مِيقَاتَ أَرْضِهِ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ أَوْ دَخَلَ مَكَّةَ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 76 – The one who exceeds the land of a juncture without Ihraam, or enters Makkah without Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/245)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/245)
text: رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ أَوْ جَهِلَ وَ قَدْ شَهِدَ الْمَنَاسِكَ كُلَّهَا وَ طَافَ وَ سَعَى قَالَ تُجْزِئُهُ نِيَّتُهُ إِذَا كَانَ قَدْ نَوَى ذَلِكَ فَقَدْ تَمَّ حَجُّهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يُهِلَّ وَ قَالَ فِي مَرِيضٍ أُغْمِيَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى أَتَى الْوَقْتَ فَقَالَ يُحْرِمُ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel Bin Darraj, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5th or 6th Imamasws) regarding a man who forgot that he should be wearing Ihraam, or did not know, and he had witnessed the rituals, all of them, and circumambulated, and performed Sa’ee. Heasws said: ‘His intention would suffice him when he had intended that. Thus, his Hajj would be complete, and even if he had not exclaimed Talbiyya’. And heasws said regarding a man who had fainted until he came to the juncture. So heasws said: ‘He should wear Ihraam from it’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “If one forgets to assume the state of Ihram or is ignorant, attends all places for performing the acts of al-Hajj such as Tawaf, Sa‘y between al-Safa’ and al-Marwah and so on, one of the two Imam, (abu Ja’far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘His intention is sufficient. If he had such intention, his Hajj is complete even though he did not say Talbiyah.’ About the case of a man who fainted due to illness until Miqat (place for assuming the state of Ihram), he (the Imam) said, ‘He must assume the state of Ihram.’”
